title: lonicera venulosa subsp. edulis
intro: Lonicera venulosa subsp. edulis, also known as the edible honeysuckle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its leaves, stems, and fruits. The health benefits of Lonicera venulosa subsp. edulis include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea, made by infusing its leaves or stems in hot water, or it can be used to make a syrup or tincture for its medicinal properties.
